report_metadata.cancer_type: HPV-Positive Oropharyngeal Squamous Cell Carcinoma in HIV/AIDS Patient

treatments.name: Concurrent cisplatin + IMRT (70 Gy) with INSTI-based ART optimization

evidence_level.rationale: RTOG 1016 and ASTRO 2024 guidelines confirm cisplatin + RT as standard of care for HPV+ OPC. 70 Gy with concurrent cisplatin remains gold standard. T2N1 is borderline for concurrent systemic therapy (single node <=3cm may be RT alone).

survival_benefit.rationale: 5-year OS 84.6% with cisplatin + RT in RTOG 1016 for HPV+ OPC. HIV-positive patients have worse outcomes (greater hazards for recurrence and death), making intensified therapy important. Standard CRT remains superior to de-intensification.

accessibility.rationale: Cisplatin and IMRT universally available. Standard regimen at all oncology centers. ART optimization with INSTI-based regimen minimizes drug interactions.

safety_profile.rationale: Must switch from TDF to TAF or abacavir to avoid compounding cisplatin nephrotoxicity. Overlapping toxicities: cisplatin nephrotoxicity + TDF renal effects, NRTI neuropathy + cisplatin neuropathy. CD4 will drop ~200 cells during CRT. Enhanced mucositis monitoring needed.

biomarker_match.rationale: HPV+ tumors are highly radiosensitive. PD-L1 CPS>=20 indicates robust immune microenvironment. CD4 350 is adequate for treatment tolerance. Undetectable VL indicates good ART adherence.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Cisplatin forms DNA crosslinks that impair repair of radiation-induced double-strand breaks, providing radiosensitization. IMRT delivers 70 Gy to gross disease with conformal dose distribution. ART must be optimized to INSTI-based backbone (dolutegravir/bictegravir + TAF/emtricitabine) to minimize drug interactions and overlapping nephrotoxicity with cisplatin.

key_evidence.study_name: NRG Oncology RTOG 1016 / ASTRO HPV+ OPC Guideline 2024

notable_side_effects: Nephrotoxicity (compounded by TDF if not switched)
CD4 decline during treatment (~200 cell drop)

treatments.name: Pembrolizumab + cisplatin/5-FU (KEYNOTE-048 regimen) adapted for HIV

evidence_level.rationale: KEYNOTE-048 demonstrated OS benefit with pembrolizumab + chemo in CPS>=20 HNSCC (median OS 14.7 vs 11.0 months, HR 0.60). Phase 1 CITN-12 trial confirmed pembrolizumab safety in HIV+ patients with CD4>=100. Not standard for locally advanced disease but relevant for this patient's PD-L1 status.

survival_benefit.rationale: In CPS>=20 R/M HNSCC, pembrolizumab + chemo showed 14.7 months median OS vs 11.0 months. For locally advanced disease, KEYNOTE-689 showed perioperative benefit. 5-year OS 16% for pembrolizumab-chemo vs 5.2% for EXTREME in total population.

accessibility.rationale: Pembrolizumab FDA-approved for first-line R/M HNSCC. For curative-intent locally advanced disease, use is per KEYNOTE-689 perioperative indication. HIV was historically exclusionary but increasing inclusion in trials.

safety_profile.rationale: Phase 1 CITN-12 showed pembrolizumab safe in HIV with CD4>=100. CD4 counts remained stable. No HIV viral rebound. irAEs comparable to HIV-negative patients (~20%). Risk of immune flare but no documented IRIS cases with anti-PD-1 in HIV.

biomarker_match.rationale: PD-L1 CPS>=20 is the strongest predictor of pembrolizumab benefit. HPV+ tumors have high TIL infiltration and PD-1 expression. HIV-induced chronic immune activation may paradoxically enhance checkpoint inhibitor response.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Pembrolizumab blocks PD-1/PD-L1 checkpoint, restoring T-cell anti-tumor immunity. In HIV+ patients, PD-1 blockade may provide dual benefit: anti-tumor activity and potential enhancement of HIV-specific immune responses. Combined with cisplatin/5-FU for cytotoxic synergy. CD4 T-cells remain stable during anti-PD-1 therapy.

key_evidence.journal: Journal of Clinical Oncology / JAMA Oncology

treatments.name: Perioperative pembrolizumab + surgery + adjuvant RT (KEYNOTE-689 adapted)

evidence_level.rationale: KEYNOTE-689 showed EFS benefit with perioperative pembrolizumab in resectable locally advanced HNSCC (HR 0.66, p=0.004 for CPS>=10). FDA approved June 2025. T2N1 is potentially resectable with TORS.

survival_benefit.rationale: 36-month EFS 59.8% vs 45.9% with perioperative pembrolizumab. CPS>=10 subgroup (patient has CPS>=20) showed strongest benefit. HIV-positive patients may benefit particularly from immune priming.

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for resectable locally advanced HNSCC with PD-L1 CPS>=1. Requires surgical expertise (TORS) and immunotherapy coordination. HIV status may complicate clinical trial access.

safety_profile.rationale: Pembrolizumab safe in HIV+ patients per CITN-12. Surgical risks standard. Adjuvant RT toxicity manageable. irAEs require close monitoring given immunocompromised state. CD4 stability demonstrated.

biomarker_match.rationale: PD-L1 CPS>=20 exceeds the CPS>=10 threshold for strongest KEYNOTE-689 benefit. HPV+ tumors with high PD-L1 and TILs are ideal candidates for perioperative immunotherapy.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Neoadjuvant pembrolizumab primes anti-tumor immune response against intact tumor, potentially expanding tumor-specific T-cell clones. Surgery removes bulk disease. Adjuvant pembrolizumab with RT maintains immune surveillance against micrometastatic disease.

treatments.name: IMRT alone (70 Gy) without concurrent chemotherapy

evidence_level.rationale: ASTRO 2024 guidelines state RT alone is appropriate for T2N1 with single node <=3cm. Avoids all chemotherapy drug interactions with ART. Multiple series show excellent outcomes for early-stage HPV+ OPC with RT alone.

survival_benefit.rationale: RT alone achieves excellent local control for T1-2N0-1 HPV+ OPC. May sacrifice some benefit of concurrent systemic therapy but avoids chemotherapy toxicity that may be amplified in HIV setting.

accessibility.rationale: IMRT universally available. No drug interaction concerns with ART. Simplifies management in HIV/cancer co-management setting.

safety_profile.rationale: Eliminates cisplatin nephrotoxicity risk overlapping with TDF. No myelosuppression from chemotherapy. HIV+ patients tolerate modern IMRT comparably to HIV-negative patients. Grade 3 mucositis ~17% in HIV+ patients with RT alone.

biomarker_match.rationale: HPV+ tumors are exquisitely radiosensitive. High cure rates with RT alone in favorable-risk disease. CD4 350 adequate for radiation tolerance.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Definitive IMRT delivers 70 Gy in 33-35 fractions to gross disease with conformal dose distribution, exploiting the enhanced radiosensitivity of HPV-positive tumors. Avoids all chemotherapy-ART drug interactions and overlapping toxicities.

key_evidence.study_name: ASTRO HPV+ OPC Clinical Practice Guideline

treatments.name: Concurrent carboplatin/5-FU + IMRT (cisplatin-ineligible alternative)

evidence_level.rationale: ASTRO 2024 guidelines conditionally recommend carboplatin/5-FU as alternative when cisplatin is contraindicated. Less nephrotoxic than cisplatin, reducing overlap with TDF toxicity.

survival_benefit.rationale: Carboplatin/5-FU provides radiosensitization comparable to cisplatin in some series but with less definitive phase 3 data in HPV+ OPC specifically. Reasonable alternative when cisplatin poses unacceptable renal risk.

accessibility.rationale: Carboplatin and 5-FU widely available. Standard alternative regimen at all oncology centers.

safety_profile.rationale: Less nephrotoxic than cisplatin (important for TDF co-administration). Less ototoxic. Still causes myelosuppression which may worsen HIV-related cytopenias. 5-FU mucositis adds to radiation effect.

biomarker_match.rationale: HPV+ tumors respond to platinum-based radiosensitization regardless of specific platinum agent. Provides chemotherapy benefit with improved renal safety profile for HIV patients on TDF.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Carboplatin provides DNA crosslinking for radiosensitization with less renal tubular toxicity than cisplatin. 5-FU inhibits thymidylate synthase, providing additional radiosensitization. Combination is preferred when cisplatin poses unacceptable nephrotoxicity risk from TDF co-administration.

treatments.name: TORS + selective neck dissection + risk-adapted adjuvant therapy

evidence_level.rationale: ECOG 3311 and ASCO 2024 TORS guideline support surgery-first approach for T1-T2 OPC. Pathology-guided adjuvant therapy minimizes overtreatment. Avoids concurrent chemo-ART interactions.

survival_benefit.rationale: ECOG 3311 showed >90% 2-year PFS across all risk groups. Surgery-first approach allows pathologic risk stratification: low risk = observation, intermediate = de-escalated RT, high risk = CRT.

accessibility.rationale: TORS requires specialized robotic surgery center. Not available at all institutions. May require referral for HIV/cancer co-management expertise.

safety_profile.rationale: Surgical approach avoids concurrent chemotherapy-ART interactions. If adjuvant RT alone sufficient (intermediate risk), no chemotherapy needed. Surgical complications manageable in HIV+ patients with adequate CD4.

biomarker_match.rationale: HPV+ tumors are well-suited for surgical de-escalation approach. Pathologic staging provides definitive risk stratification regardless of HIV status.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Transoral robotic surgery with ipsilateral neck dissection provides primary treatment and definitive pathologic staging. Pathology guides adjuvant therapy: negative margins + limited nodal disease = observation or de-escalated RT; adverse features = standard adjuvant therapy. Avoids concurrent chemotherapy-ART interactions.

treatments.name: ART regimen optimization to INSTI-based backbone prior to oncologic treatment

evidence_level.rationale: Expert consensus and pharmacokinetic studies recommend INSTI-based ART (dolutegravir or bictegravir) during chemotherapy due to minimal CYP450 drug interactions. NIH guidelines endorse ART modification for cancer treatment.

survival_benefit.rationale: Prevents treatment-limiting drug interactions that could compromise either ART efficacy (HIV rebound) or chemotherapy dosing (altered metabolism). Maintains undetectable VL throughout cancer treatment.

accessibility.rationale: INSTI-based regimens (e.g., Biktarvy, Tivicay) are widely available and first-line ART options. Simple switch for most patients.

safety_profile.rationale: INSTI-based regimens have favorable safety profiles. Dolutegravir and bictegravir have minimal CYP interactions with chemotherapy. Switching from TDF to TAF eliminates overlapping nephrotoxicity with cisplatin.

biomarker_match.rationale: Patient has undetectable VL and CD4 350, indicating good virologic control. INSTI-based regimen maintains virologic suppression while enabling full-dose oncologic therapy.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Switching to an INSTI-based ART backbone (dolutegravir or bictegravir + TAF/FTC) eliminates CYP3A4-mediated drug interactions between protease inhibitors/NNRTIs and chemotherapy agents. Switching from TDF to TAF reduces overlapping nephrotoxicity with cisplatin. INSTIs are metabolized via UGT1A1 with minimal CYP450 interaction.

key_evidence.study_name: Management of HIV Infection in Patients with Cancer Receiving Chemotherapy

treatments.name: Nivolumab salvage therapy (for recurrence)

evidence_level.rationale: CheckMate 141 demonstrated OS benefit in R/M HNSCC after platinum progression. HPV+ subgroup showed particularly strong benefit (OS 9.1 vs 4.1 months). Safe in HIV+ patients based on multiple retrospective series.

survival_benefit.rationale: Relevant only as salvage if primary treatment fails. OS 9.1 months in HPV+ R/M patients. Strong salvage option that preserves quality of life. HIV+ patients with CD4>=100 can receive nivolumab.

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for R/M HNSCC after platinum failure. Widely available. No significant ART drug interactions.

safety_profile.rationale: Well tolerated in HIV+ patients. CD4 counts remain stable. No HIV viral rebound. irAEs similar to HIV-negative patients. Grade 3-4 AEs in ~15%.

biomarker_match.rationale: HPV+ tumors showed superior nivolumab response. PD-L1 CPS>=20 predicts enhanced checkpoint inhibitor efficacy. HIV-associated PD-1 upregulation may paradoxically prime for anti-PD-1 response.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Nivolumab blocks PD-1 checkpoint, restoring anti-tumor T-cell function. In HIV+ patients, may provide dual anti-tumor and anti-HIV immune restoration. HPV+ tumors with high TIL density and PD-1 expression are particularly responsive.

treatments.name: Pembrolizumab monotherapy (CPS>=20, curative-intent concurrent with RT)

evidence_level.rationale: Pembrolizumab concurrent with RT is being studied in multiple trials for locally advanced HNSCC. KEYNOTE-412 did not meet primary endpoint for unselected population. CPS>=20 subgroup may show benefit. No HIV-specific data.

survival_benefit.rationale: Theoretical benefit of combining anti-PD-1 with RT-induced immunogenic cell death. CPS>=20 enriches response probability. Avoids chemotherapy-ART interactions entirely.

accessibility.rationale: Pembrolizumab FDA approved for HNSCC. Off-label for concurrent RT use in locally advanced setting. Clinical trial access preferred.

safety_profile.rationale: Avoids all chemotherapy-ART drug interactions. Pembrolizumab safe in HIV+ per CITN-12. irAEs manageable. No nephrotoxicity or ototoxicity concerns.

biomarker_match.rationale: PD-L1 CPS>=20 is the strongest predictor of pembrolizumab benefit. HPV+ tumor with high TIL density. RT-induced immunogenic cell death may synergize with anti-PD-1. Avoids ART interactions.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Pembrolizumab concurrent with radiation leverages RT-induced immunogenic cell death to release tumor neoantigens while anti-PD-1 blockade prevents T-cell exhaustion. Particularly attractive in HIV+ patients because it avoids all chemotherapy-ART drug interactions.

treatments.name: Cetuximab + RT (bioradiotherapy - NOT recommended)

evidence_level.rationale: RTOG 1016 and De-ESCALaTE definitively showed cetuximab + RT is INFERIOR to cisplatin + RT in HPV+ OPC. This approach should NOT be used as de-escalation strategy.

survival_benefit.rationale: 5-year OS 77.9% with cetuximab vs 84.6% with cisplatin (RTOG 1016). 2-year OS 89.4% vs 97.5% in De-ESCALaTE. Significantly worse outcomes regardless of HIV status.

accessibility.rationale: Cetuximab widely available. No significant ART drug interactions (EGFR inhibitor metabolism is distinct from CYP450 pathways used by PIs/NNRTIs).

safety_profile.rationale: Similar overall toxicity to cisplatin + RT. Acneiform rash. Infusion reactions. No advantage in toxicity despite inferior efficacy. Not justified in HIV+ patients.

biomarker_match.rationale: HPV+ tumors may have different EGFR biology rendering cetuximab less effective. Not matched to any actionable biomarker in this patient.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Cetuximab is an anti-EGFR monoclonal antibody originally hypothesized as a less toxic radiosensitizer for HPV+ OPC. RTOG 1016 and De-ESCALaTE proved this approach inferior to cisplatin-based CRT with similar toxicity.
treatments.availability: FDA Approved (NOT recommended for HPV+ OPC)

treatments.name: Broadly neutralizing HIV antibodies (bNAbs) to replace ART during chemotherapy

evidence_level.rationale: Preclinical and early clinical data suggest bNAbs could maintain HIV suppression while eliminating ART drug interactions during chemotherapy. Phase 1/2 trials ongoing. Novel approach.

survival_benefit.rationale: Theoretical benefit of enabling full-dose chemotherapy without ART drug interactions. Could improve both cancer and HIV outcomes by allowing unconstrained oncologic treatment.

accessibility.rationale: Experimental. Available only in clinical trials at major academic centers. Not FDA approved for ART replacement during cancer treatment.

safety_profile.rationale: bNAbs have favorable safety profiles. Infusion reactions possible. Risk of HIV viral rebound if bNAb coverage insufficient. Requires careful HIV monitoring.

biomarker_match.rationale: Requires HIV sensitivity testing to selected bNAbs. Not all HIV strains are susceptible. Patient must have undetectable VL and adequate CD4 for safe ART interruption.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Broadly neutralizing HIV antibodies (e.g., VRC01, 10-1074, 3BNC117) provide passive immunization against HIV, maintaining viral suppression without oral ART. Eliminates all ART-chemotherapy drug interactions, enabling unconstrained oncologic dosing.

key_evidence.study_name: Replacement of ART with HIV bNAbs during chemotherapy (concept paper)

treatments.name: ISA101b HPV16 therapeutic vaccine + anti-PD-1 (investigational)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 OpcemISA trial ongoing for R/M HPV16+ OPC. ORR 11.5% in anti-PD-1-refractory patients. No data in HIV+ population or locally advanced setting.

survival_benefit.rationale: Studied only in recurrent/metastatic setting. Theoretical synergy with HIV-related immune dysregulation (chronic HPV exposure may enhance vaccine response with checkpoint blockade).

accessibility.rationale: Clinical trial only. HIV+ patients historically excluded from most trials though inclusion is expanding. FDA Fast Track designation.

safety_profile.rationale: Well tolerated in HIV-negative patients. Safety in HIV+ unknown but mechanistic rationale suggests acceptable risk. HPV-specific immune boosting unlikely to cause autoimmunity.

biomarker_match.rationale: Targets HPV16 E6/E7 oncoproteins. Requires HPV16 confirmation. HIV/HPV co-infected patients may have impaired HPV-specific T-cell responses that could be restored by vaccine + anti-PD-1.

treatments.mechanism_of_action: ISA101b induces HPV16-specific CD4+ and CD8+ T-cell responses targeting E6/E7 oncoproteins. Combined with anti-PD-1 to overcome immune exhaustion. In HIV+ patients, could restore HPV-specific immunity that is impaired by chronic immunosuppression.

clinical_trials.title: Phase 1 Assessment of Safety of Pembrolizumab in Patients with HIV and Advanced Cancer
clinical_trials.early_results: Pembrolizumab safe in HIV+ cancer patients. CD4 counts stable. No viral rebound. irAEs comparable to HIV-negative patients.

clinical_trials.title: Neoadjuvant and Adjuvant Pembrolizumab in Locally Advanced Head and Neck Cancer
clinical_trials.early_results: 36-month EFS 59.8% vs 45.9% (HR 0.66, p=0.004) in CPS>=10. FDA approved June 2025.

clinical_trials.title: IFCT-CHIVA2: Nivolumab After Chemotherapy in HIV-Positive Patients with Advanced NSCLC
clinical_trials.early_results: Evaluating nivolumab safety and efficacy in HIV+ cancer population.

clinical_trials.title: OpcemISA: Cemiplimab +/- ISA101b in HPV16+ OPC
clinical_trials.early_results: ORR 11.5% in anti-PD-1-refractory. Clinical benefit ratio 61.5%.

clinical_trials.title: Cancer Immunotherapy Trials Network: Immune Checkpoint Inhibitors in HIV-Associated Cancers
clinical_trials.early_results: Expanding evidence for safety of ICIs in HIV+ patients across tumor types.

clinical_trials.title: Pembrolizumab + RT De-escalation in HPV+ OPC (inclusive of HIV+)
clinical_trials.early_results: Evaluating pembrolizumab + reduced-dose RT as alternative to CRT.

combination_strategies.rationale: INSTI-based ART has minimal CYP450 drug interactions with cisplatin. Switching from TDF to TAF eliminates overlapping nephrotoxicity. INSTIs metabolized via UGT1A1, distinct from cisplatin metabolism.

Pembrolizumab safe in HIV+ patients (CITN-12). No ART drug interactions. PD-1 blockade may restore both anti-tumor and anti-HIV immunity. RT-induced immunogenic cell death synergizes with checkpoint blockade.

combination_strategies.combination_partner: De-escalated adjuvant RT (50 Gy) without chemotherapy
combination_strategies.rationale: Surgery-first approach provides definitive staging. De-escalated RT without chemotherapy avoids all ART-chemo drug interactions. Particularly attractive for HIV+ patients where drug interactions complicate CRT.
Carboplatin less nephrotoxic than cisplatin, reducing overlap with TDF. TAF has improved renal safety vs TDF. Combination provides radiosensitization with manageable renal toxicity profile.

supportive_care.approach: ART optimization by HIV specialist prior to oncologic treatment
supportive_care.evidence: Expert consensus recommends INSTI-based ART during chemotherapy to minimize drug interactions. TDF-to-TAF switch reduces nephrotoxicity overlap with cisplatin.
supportive_care.benefit: Prevents treatment-limiting drug interactions, maintains HIV suppression, enables full-dose chemotherapy

supportive_care.approach: CD4 and HIV viral load monitoring during cancer treatment
supportive_care.evidence: CD4 drops ~200 cells during CRT. Viral rebound risk with immunosuppression. Regular monitoring (every 3-4 weeks) guides ART adjustment and opportunistic infection prophylaxis.
supportive_care.benefit: Early detection of immune deterioration, prevents opportunistic infections, ensures ART efficacy

supportive_care.evidence: CD4 may drop below 200 during CRT. Pneumocystis prophylaxis (TMP-SMX) recommended when CD4 <200. MAC prophylaxis if CD4 <50.
supportive_care.benefit: Prevents life-threatening opportunistic infections during treatment-induced immunosuppression

supportive_care.approach: Enhanced nutritional support and mucositis management
supportive_care.evidence: HIV+ patients may have baseline nutritional deficiency. CRT-induced mucositis can worsen oral intake. Proactive PEG tube consideration if pre-treatment weight loss >5%.
supportive_care.benefit: Maintains treatment intensity, prevents treatment breaks, reduces morbidity

supportive_care.approach: Multidisciplinary team coordination (oncology, infectious disease, pharmacy)
supportive_care.evidence: Drug interaction management, ART optimization, and immune monitoring require coordinated care. Studies show improved outcomes with multidisciplinary management of HIV-cancer comorbidity.
supportive_care.benefit: Optimal drug selection, reduced complications, comprehensive immune management

supportive_care.approach: Psychosocial support for dual HIV/cancer diagnosis
supportive_care.evidence: Patients with HIV and cancer face compounded stigma, treatment burden, and psychological distress. Mental health support improves adherence to both ART and cancer treatment.
supportive_care.benefit: Improved treatment adherence, reduced distress, better quality of life
